quote: SIP is not about timing the market. It's about time IN the market. Do it every month like you pay rent — because your future self deserves that discipline.
context: On systematic investment plans and the power of consistency over smartness
krishna_angle: Karma Yoga of investing — do your duty every month, release attachment to daily NAV

quote: Before you invest a single rupee, build your emergency fund. 6 months of expenses, liquid, untouched. That is not fear — that is wisdom.
context: On financial foundations and why emergency fund comes before any investment
krishna_angle: The Kavach — your protective armour before riding into the battlefield of markets

quote: Term insurance is the most powerful financial product most people ignore. Pure protection, lowest cost, maximum cover. If you have dependents and no term plan, you are gambling with their future.
context: On why term insurance is non-negotiable for anyone with financial dependents
krishna_angle: The Sudarshana Chakra — simple, decisive, all-protecting. Don't complicate what just needs to work.

quote: Invest for your goals, not for returns. The moment you chase returns, you've already lost your compass. Define the goal — child's education, home, retirement — then work backwards.
context: On goal-based financial planning as opposed to return-chasing
krishna_angle: Arjuna aimed at the eye of the fish — one clear goal, not the entire bird. Focus creates results.

quote: Financial freedom means your money works while you sleep. It is not about being rich — it is about never being trapped by the fear of not having enough.
context: On redefining wealth as freedom rather than accumulation
krishna_angle: The ultimate Moksha of personal finance — freedom from financial anxiety is the highest return

quote: The best time to start investing was yesterday. The second best time is today with whatever you have — even ₹500. Compounding doesn't care about the amount, it cares about the time.
context: On overcoming the mental barrier of 'not having enough' to start investing
krishna_angle: Don't wait for the perfect moment, Parth. The chariot moves when you pick up the reins, not when you feel ready.

quote: Mutual funds are the great equaliser. The same basket of Sensex stocks that a billionaire holds — you can hold too, with ₹1000 a month. Don't let complexity steal your access to good investments.
context: On mutual funds as democratised investing for middle-class India
krishna_angle: The divine economy — everyone on the battlefield of wealth deserves the same weapons, not just the kings

quote: Asset allocation is your battlefield strategy. Equity for growth, debt for stability, gold for the unpredictable. Know your timeline, know your temperament, then decide the mix.
context: On the importance of asset allocation matching risk appetite and time horizon
krishna_angle: Kurukshetra strategy — every army on the right flank. Equity soldiers in front, debt as reserves, gold as the wild card.

quote: The best financial plan is the one you will actually follow. Complexity is the enemy of action. Two mutual funds, one SIP, one term plan — that simple portfolio beats the 'sophisticated' one the investor abandoned.
context: On simplicity as a financial virtue — the plan you follow beats the perfect plan you don't
krishna_angle: Gita in 18 chapters, essence in one shloka. Your entire financial plan should fit on one page.

quote: Market corrections are not the enemy — they are the discount sale. The investor who panics and exits is the one who bought at the top and sold at the bottom. Stay. The market recovers. It always has.
context: On staying invested through market downturns and corrections
krishna_angle: The battle has dark moments. The warrior who retreats in the first hour loses. The one who holds position wins.

quote: Bad debt is the compound interest working against you. Pay off high-interest debt — credit cards, personal loans — before you invest a single rupee in equity. The math is brutal on both sides.
context: On prioritising debt repayment especially high-interest consumer debt
krishna_angle: You cannot fight the battle carrying shackles. Clear the debt, then ride free into the wealth battlefield.

quote: Rebalance your portfolio once a year. That is it. No more, no less. Your asset allocation drifts with markets — bring it back. This one annual act prevents years of regret.
context: On portfolio rebalancing as a simple but powerful annual practice
krishna_angle: Even the Sudarshana Chakra returns to the hand. Your portfolio needs to come back to its intended balance.

quote: The stock market is a device for transferring money from the impatient to the patient.
context: On patience as the primary competitive advantage of the individual investor
krishna_angle: Buffett sat on the chariot for decades without swinging his sword unnecessarily. That patience is his superpower.

quote: Be fearful when others are greedy, and greedy when others are fearful.
context: On contrarian thinking and using market emotions as signals
krishna_angle: When everyone dropped their bow in fear, Arjuna picked his up. The crash is when the warrior moves.

quote: Never invest in a business you cannot understand.
context: On staying within your circle of competence and avoiding complexity traps
krishna_angle: Know your battlefield. Arjuna knew archery — he didn't try to fight like Bhima. Invest in what you understand.

quote: If you aren't willing to own a stock for ten years, don't even think about owning it for ten minutes.
context: On the long-term mindset as a prerequisite for equity investing
krishna_angle: Commit to the battlefield, Parth. Half-hearted warriors lose. Full commitment — then the outcome takes care of itself.

quote: Risk comes from not knowing what you are doing.
context: On how ignorance — not markets — is the true source of investment risk
krishna_angle: The unarmed warrior is the dangerous one — not because the enemy is strong, but because he himself is unprepared.

quote: Invert, always invert. Tell me where I'm going to die, so I'll never go there.
context: On inverting problems — instead of asking how to get rich, ask how people go broke
krishna_angle: Krishna didn't just show Arjuna the path to victory — he showed him every path to defeat first. Know what kills before you charge.

quote: The first rule of compounding: never interrupt it unnecessarily.
context: On why staying invested and not redeeming early is the single most important rule
krishna_angle: The Banyan tree doesn't ask to be checked every week. You plant it, you water it, you leave it alone. That is compounding.

quote: I have nothing to add — said after Buffett spoke. The best investors know when to be quiet and let compounding speak.
context: On the wisdom of simplicity and silence in investing — less action is often better
krishna_angle: The Gita's most powerful moment: Krishna stops talking and lets Arjuna decide. Sometimes the best move is stillness.

quote: Invest in what you know. Your best investment idea might be sitting right in your neighbourhood or in your shopping cart.
context: On using everyday consumer experience as a source of investment insight
krishna_angle: Arjuna was the greatest archer because he practiced where he stood. Your edge comes from your own life's observations.

quote: The real key to making money in stocks is not to get scared out of them.
context: On emotional resilience as the investor's most important trait
krishna_angle: Parth's biggest battle wasn't against Karna — it was against his own fear on day one. Same for every investor.

quote: Mr. Market is your servant, not your master. When he panics and offers you a great business at a silly price, thank him and buy.
context: On the Mr. Market parable and using market irrationality to your advantage
krishna_angle: The marketplace of the world goes mad sometimes. The calm warrior sees opportunity where the crowd sees catastrophe.

quote: The margin of safety is the central concept of investment. Never pay the full price when a discount protects you from being wrong.
context: On margin of safety as a fundamental investment principle
krishna_angle: A warrior doesn't walk into battle without armour. The margin of safety is your financial armour — always wear it.

quote: Karmanye vadhikaraste ma phaleshu kadachana — You have a right to perform your prescribed duties, but you are not entitled to the fruits of those actions.
context: Chapter 2, Verse 47 — the most famous verse on detachment from outcomes
krishna_angle: Do your SIP every month. Don't obsess over the NAV. The fruit will come — but the karma (action) is yours to control.

quote: Arjuna, why do you grieve? Rise, perform your duty. The battlefield waits for no one.
context: On analysis paralysis — the paralysis before action that costs more than any bad decision
krishna_angle: The investor who waits for the perfect entry point never enters. Parth, pick up the bow.

quote: Treat pleasure and pain, gain and loss, victory and defeat with equanimity.
context: On maintaining emotional equanimity through bull and bear markets alike
krishna_angle: The market will give you both highs and lows. The investor who stays equally calm through both — that is the true wealth warrior.

quote: It is better to strive in one's own dharma than to succeed in the dharma of another.
context: On staying true to your own financial goals and not chasing what others are investing in
krishna_angle: Your neighbour bought crypto and made money. Your brother-in-law is in real estate. Your dharma is YOUR financial goal — not theirs.

quote: Knowledge without action is as useless as a lamp that gives no light.
context: On the gap between financial knowledge and financial action — information alone does not build wealth
krishna_angle: Knowing the Gita and living the Gita are two different things. Knowing about SIPs and starting one — same difference.

quote: Asset allocation is like building a cricket team. You need batsmen, bowlers, and all-rounders. A team of 11 batsmen looks great in theory but loses every match. Equity is your batting order, debt is your defence, gold is your wild card.
context: On why balanced asset allocation always beats concentration in one asset class
krishna_angle: The Pandava army had warriors of every kind — that is why they won. One weapon type never wins a war.

quote: Markets are always right in the long run, even if they are wrong in the short run. The investor's job is not to be smarter than the market today — it is to still be invested when the market is right tomorrow.
context: On long-term faith in markets versus short-term noise and panic
krishna_angle: Krishna knew the outcome of Kurukshetra from day one. The warriors did not. Their job was to show up and fight — not predict which hour they would win.

quote: The biggest risk in investing is not market risk. It is investor behaviour risk — the risk that you will do the wrong thing at the wrong time because of fear or greed.
context: On how investor emotions cause more damage than market volatility ever could
krishna_angle: Arjuna's real enemy on day one was not Karna or Drona. It was his own mind. Same for every investor.

quote: Equity is for goals that are 5 years or more away. Debt is for goals within 3 years. Hybrid for goals in between. This one rule, followed consistently, prevents 90% of investing mistakes.
context: On matching investment instruments to goal timelines
krishna_angle: You do not use a long bow for close combat or a sword for distance archery. Match your weapon to your battlefield distance.

quote: SIP forces you to invest when you do not feel like it. That is exactly when investing matters most.
context: On SIP as a discipline mechanism that removes emotion from investing decisions
krishna_angle: A warrior who trains only when motivated loses. One who trains every day regardless of mood — that warrior wins Kurukshetra.

quote: Gold is insurance, not an investment. It protects your portfolio when everything else falls. But insurance that becomes your entire portfolio is a different kind of risk.
context: On gold's correct role in a portfolio — hedge and protection, not primary wealth builder
krishna_angle: Your armour protects you in battle — but you cannot walk into Kurukshetra wearing only armour. You need a bow too.

quote: Before asking what to invest in, ask who is investing. A 25-year-old with no EMIs and a stable job can take risks a 50-year-old with three EMIs and school fees cannot. Same product, completely different answer.
context: On why risk profiling and personal context must come before any investment recommendation
krishna_angle: Krishna gave Arjuna a personalised Gita — not a general lecture. He knew Arjuna's fears, his family, his dharma. That is why it worked.

quote: Tell me your goal before you tell me where you want to invest. The goal is the destination. The investment is just the vehicle. You do not buy a vehicle before you know where you are going.
context: On the absolute primacy of goal definition before product selection
krishna_angle: Arjuna did not pick up his bow and then decide what he was fighting for. He knew his dharma first. Then he picked up the bow.

quote: The best guaranteed return in India is eliminating 18% credit card interest. Pay off high-interest debt before you invest a single rupee in equity.
context: On why high-interest debt repayment is the single best investment for most people carrying it
krishna_angle: You cannot run into battle with a weight tied to your leg. Clear the debt. Then invest. In that order.

quote: Do not yield to impotence, Arjuna. It does not become you. Shake off your faint-heartedness and arise. — Chapter 2, Verse 3
context: Krishna snapping Arjuna out of paralysis — the original stop-overthinking-and-start moment
krishna_angle: Every investor who waits for the right time to start their SIP is Arjuna sitting in the chariot, not fighting. Arise, Parth.

quote: Let a man lift himself by his own self alone, let him not lower himself — for this self alone is the friend of oneself and the enemy of oneself. — Chapter 6, Verse 5
context: On self-discipline as the source of both financial success and failure
krishna_angle: The investor who follows their SIP and stays the course is their own best friend. The one who panic-sells is their own enemy.

quote: Among all remedies, knowledge is the most purifying. — Chapter 4, Verse 38
context: On why financial literacy is the foundation of all good investment decisions
krishna_angle: The investor who understands what they own and why will never panic. Financial knowledge is the antidote to market fear.

quote: Yoga is not for one who eats too much or too little, nor for one who sleeps too much or stays awake too much. — Chapter 6, Verse 16
context: On balance and the middle path — applied to investing it means steady contributions, not feast-or-famine behaviour
krishna_angle: Don't put ₹50,000 in markets when excited and zero when markets fall. The middle path — steady SIP every month — is the investor's yoga.
